commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Savrin Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Savrin's 4G-series petrol engines are generally robust but age-related failures are common on grey imports. Overheating on 4G93 units and oil leaks on 4G64 engines dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Savrin 1.8 Overheating - Why 4G93 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Ageing thermostat sticks closed or water pump impeller corrodes, causing coolant circulation failure and head gasket stress.

repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before overheating; prevents head damage
Temporary fix; underlying block stress may cause repeat failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded seals, bench tested
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a Savrin valued £2,000-£4,000, preventative coolant service is cost-effective if symptoms appear early. Once overheating has occurred, head warpage is likely - a rebuilt 4G93 with upgraded components offers better value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G93 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Savrin

problems.h4: Savrin 2.4 Oil Leak - 4G64 Seal Failure Explained
problems.rootCause: Valve cover gasket hardens with heat cycles; rear main seal wears from crankshaft movement, allowing oil seepage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Gearbox removal, seal replacement, clutch inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Labour-intensive; consider engine replacement if other issues present

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with all seals renewed, dyno tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vehicles; eliminates multiple failure points

problems.recommendation: For isolated valve cover leaks, gasket replacement is sensible. If rear main seal leakage coincides with other age-related issues, a rebuilt 4G64 offers comprehensive peace of mind at competitive pricing.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi Savrin engine replacement

problems.h4: Savrin Timing Belt Failure - 4G93 Interference Engine Risk
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner fails or belt teeth shear; as an interference engine, piston-valve contact causes catastrophic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit after belt-snap valve damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once interference damage has occurred

problems.recommendation: The 4G93 is an interference engine - belt replacement at 60k miles is non-negotiable. If the belt has snapped, valve damage is certain; a rebuilt engine is the only viable repair path.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G93 engine prices after timing belt failure

problems.h4: Savrin Rough Idle/Misfire - Ignition System Failures
problems.rootCause: Coil packs degrade with heat exposure; ignition leads crack; spark plugs foul - causing cylinder misfire and rough running.

repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated misfire; diagnostic essential first

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: All ignition components plus ECU diagnostic
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive fix for persistent misfire issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if misfire caused secondary damage (e.g., catalytic converter)
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed for ignition issues alone; consider only if other failures present

problems.recommendation: Start with professional diagnostics to isolate the faulty component. Most misfires resolve with coil/lead replacement; engine replacement is unnecessary unless secondary damage has occurred.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi Savrin ignition repair specialists near you

problems.h4: Savrin Loss of Power - Fuel/Air System Issues
problems.rootCause: Fuel pump pressure drops, MAF sensor contamination, or exhaust restriction reduces engine breathing and power output.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: MAF clean/replacement, fuel filter, air filter
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective first step; resolves many power loss cases

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Only if power loss stems from internal compression loss
repairOptions.longevity: Rare; requires compression test confirmation before considering

problems.recommendation: Power loss is rarely engine-internal on Savrins. Begin with diagnostics to check fuel pressure, sensor readings, and exhaust flow before considering major repairs.
problems.cta: Get Mitsubishi Savrin engine diagnostics from UK specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Savrin
finalCta.paragraph: From overheating 4G93 units to oil-leaking 4G64 engines, Mitsubishi Savrin owners face specific, well-documented failure patterns. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Savrin specialists who understand grey import servicing. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Savrin Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Savrin and engine code. years.description: The original Savrin launched with 4G93 and 4G63 petrol engines, establishing the MPV's niche appeal for UK grey import buyers.

4G93 1.8L SOHC becomes the volume engine for early imports
4G63 2.0L DOHC offered in higher-spec SEI and Exceed trims
knownFor: 4G93 overheating issues emerge on high-mileage imports

keyChanges: 4G64 2.4L SOHC replaces 4G93 as the primary engine option
Revised front styling and interior updates for second generation
Inspire trim introduced with enhanced equipment levels
mainEngines: 4G63 - 2.0L petrol (2.0 Exceed, continued from first gen)

Oil seal leaks become the dominant failure mode on ageing units
Strong cross-compatibility with Space Wagon/Chariot donor engines

items.question: Where can I buy a Mitsubishi Savrin engine in the UK?
items.answer: Mitsubishi Savrin engines are available through specialist grey import suppliers and EngineMarket's network of UK Mitsubishi engine rebuilders who stock 4G93, 4G64 and 4G63 units.

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Savrin?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi Savrin uses Mitsubishi's 4G-series petrol engines: 4G93 1.8L, 4G63 2.0L and 4G64 2.4L, with cross-compatibility to Space Wagon and Chariot donors.
keyPoints: 4G93 fitted to first-gen 1.8L Savrins (1999-2004)
4G64 standard on second-gen 2.4L models (2004-2008)
4G63 appears in select 2.0L SEI/Exceed trims
Space Wagon/Chariot engines often compatible with verification
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Savrin engine compatibility guide

items.question: How much to replace a Mitsubishi Savrin engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Savrin engine prices range from £950 for a 4G93 1.8L to £2,400 for a 4G64 2.4L, supply-only, with supply & fit adding £300-£800.
keyPoints: 4G93 1.8L rebuilt: £950-£2,100 supply only
4G64 2.4L rebuilt: £1,100-£2,400 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £300-£800
Prices vary by engine condition, supplier and location
items.cta: Get a tailored Mitsubishi Savrin engine replacement quote

items.question: Is a Savrin engine the same as a Space Wagon?
items.answer: Yes, Mitsubishi Savrin engines share the 4G-series architecture with Space Wagon and Chariot models, enabling cross-compatibility for replacement sourcing.
keyPoints: 4G93, 4G63 and 4G64 codes used across multiple Mitsubishi MPVs
Mounting points and ECU may require verification for swaps
Specialist knowledge essential for grey import compatibility checks
EngineMarket specialists confirm fitment before dispatch
items.cta: Verify Mitsubishi Savrin Space Wagon engine compatibility

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Savrin 1.8 overheating?
items.answer: Overheating in Savrin 1.8L models typically stems from thermostat failure, water pump corrosion or coolant system neglect on ageing 4G93 engines.
keyPoints: Thermostat sticks closed, blocking coolant circulation
Water pump impeller corrodes after 70k+ miles
Neglected coolant changes accelerate component degradation
Overheating can warp cylinder heads on interference 4G93
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G93 engine prices for overheating Savrin

items.answer: Yes, every Mitsubishi Savrin engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across the UK
Warranty documentation provided with every rebuilt unit
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier pre-purchase
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Savrin engines with 12-month warranty UK

items.question: Will a 4G63 fit my Mitsubishi Savrin?
items.answer: The 4G63 2.0L DOHC was factory-fitted to select Savrin trims; swaps into other variants require ECU and mount verification by a Mitsubishi specialist.
keyPoints: 4G63 appeared in 2.0 SEI and Exceed Savrin models
Mounting points generally compatible within 4G family
ECU coding and sensor calibration may require adaptation
Professional fitment recommended for cross-code swaps
items.cta: Enquire about 4G63 Mitsubishi Savrin engine swap compatibility

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Savrin engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Mitsubishi Savrin 4G-series engines can exceed 150,000 miles; grey import age and service history are key longevity factors.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes and coolant service critical for 4G longevity
Timing belt replacement at 60k miles prevents catastrophic failure

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Savrin engine myself?

items.question: What causes 4G93 timing belt failure in Savrin?
items.answer: 4G93 timing belt failure typically results from missed replacement intervals, tensioner wear or oil contamination accelerating belt degradation on Mitsubishi Savrin engines.
keyPoints: Manufacturer recommends belt replacement every 60,000 miles
Tensioner bearing failure allows belt slack and tooth shear
Oil leaks onto belt accelerate rubber degradation
As an interference engine, belt snap causes valve-piston collision
